---
canvas:
  allowed_extensions:
  - pdf
  - png
  - jpg
  - jpeg
  - mp4
  - mov
  grading_type: pass_fail
  group_assignment: true
  group_set: Project Groups
  points: 1
  published: false
  submission_types:
  - online_upload
  type: assignment
title: Lab 15 – Web Server and WebSockets
---

## Learning Goals

- Create a web-based controller hosted on the ESP32
- Use WebSockets for real-time bidirectional communication
- Send joystick commands from phone, receive telemetry back
- This is the primary control method for the RC car

## Background

The ESP32 runs as a **WiFi Access Point** and hosts a web server. Your phone connects to the ESP32's WiFi and opens a page with virtual joysticks. **WebSockets** provide low-latency bidirectional communication — much faster than HTTP polling.

**Architecture:**

1. ESP32 creates WiFi AP → phone connects
2. Phone opens `http://192.168.4.1` → ESP32 serves HTML/JS page
3. WebSocket connection → real-time data exchange
4. Phone sends joystick data → ESP32 controls motor/servo
5. ESP32 sends telemetry → phone displays battery voltage

## Components

- ESP32 DevKit
- Phone or laptop with WiFi
- Motor + BTS7960 + servo (from previous labs)
- 12V power supply or 3S LiPo

## Tasks

1. Install `ESPAsyncWebServer` and `AsyncTCP` libraries.
2. Upload the starter code:

```cpp
#include <WiFi.h>
#include <AsyncTCP.h>
#include <ESPAsyncWebServer.h>

const char* ssid = "RC-Car-XX";      // Use your group number
const char* password = "mekatronik";

AsyncWebServer server(80);
AsyncWebSocket ws("/ws");

void onWebSocketEvent(AsyncWebSocket *server,
    AsyncWebSocketClient *client,
    AwsEventType type, void *arg,
    uint8_t *data, size_t len) {
  if (type == WS_EVT_DATA) {
    data[len] = 0;
    String msg = (char*)data;
    int commaIndex = msg.indexOf(',');
    if (commaIndex > 0) {
      int steering = msg.substring(0, commaIndex).toInt();
      int throttle = msg.substring(commaIndex + 1).toInt();
      Serial.print("Steer: "); Serial.print(steering);
      Serial.print(" Throt: "); Serial.println(throttle);
      // TODO: drive servo and motor here
    }
  }
}

const char webpage[] PROGMEM = R"rawliteral(
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <meta name="viewport"
        content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>RC Car</title>
  <style>
    body { font-family: sans-serif; text-align: center;
           padding: 20px; }
    .slider-group { margin: 20px; }
    input[type=range] { width: 80%; }
    #telemetry { margin-top: 20px; font-size: 1.2em; }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>RC Car Control</h1>
  <div class="slider-group">
    <label>Steering: <span id="steerVal">0</span></label><br>
    <input type="range" id="steering"
           min="-255" max="255" value="0">
  </div>
  <div class="slider-group">
    <label>Throttle: <span id="throtVal">0</span></label><br>
    <input type="range" id="throttle"
           min="-255" max="255" value="0">
  </div>
  <div id="telemetry">Battery: -- V</div>
  <script>
    const ws = new WebSocket('ws://' + location.host + '/ws');
    const steer = document.getElementById('steering');
    const throt = document.getElementById('throttle');
    function send() {
      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN)
        ws.send(steer.value + ',' + throt.value);
      document.getElementById('steerVal').textContent
        = steer.value;
      document.getElementById('throtVal').textContent
        = throt.value;
    }
    steer.addEventListener('input', send);
    throt.addEventListener('input', send);
    ws.onmessage = function(evt) {
      document.getElementById('telemetry').textContent
        = evt.data;
    };
    throt.addEventListener('mouseup', () => {
      throt.value = 0; send(); });
    throt.addEventListener('touchend', () => {
      throt.value = 0; send(); });
  </script>
</body>
</html>
)rawliteral";

void setup() {
  Serial.begin(115200);
  WiFi.softAP(ssid, password);
  Serial.print("AP IP: ");
  Serial.println(WiFi.softAPIP());
  ws.onEvent(onWebSocketEvent);
  server.addHandler(&ws);
  server.on("/", HTTP_GET, [](AsyncWebServerRequest *req) {
    req->send_P(200, "text/html", webpage);
  });
  server.begin();
}

void loop() {
  ws.cleanupClients();
  // Send telemetry every 500ms
  static unsigned long lastTx = 0;
  if (millis() - lastTx > 500) {
    float battV = analogRead(34) * 3.3 / 4095.0
                  / (10000.0 / 37000.0);
    ws.textAll("Battery: " + String(battV, 1) + " V");
    lastTx = millis();
  }
}
```

3. Connect your phone to the WiFi network. Open `http://192.168.4.1`.
4. Move the sliders — verify values on Serial Monitor.
5. Integrate motor and servo control from Labs 10–11.
6. **Project phase:** Replace sliders with touch-friendly virtual joysticks (e.g. `nipplejs` library or custom canvas).
7. Add battery telemetry from Lab 12.

## Submission

Upload a **video** showing phone-controlled motor/servo via the web interface, or a screenshot of the web page with Serial Monitor output.
